summary: Full E2E for A10 (capacity → waitlist), A11 (soft plan usage), A12 (GDPR anonymize guard). 9/9 backend pytest pass + complete waitlist UI flow verified end-to-end. Public PlanUsageBanner correctly hidden for PRO+ demo and /api/plan/usage returns the full soft-limit snapshot shape. No regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/events.py POST /{event_id}/dates Pydantic model EventDateCreate requires start_datetime + end_datetime as plain `str` — consider strict datetime with timezone validation to avoid silent malformed timestamps slipping into the DB.

Waitlist seat occupancy is enforced via pg_advisory_xact_lock + OCCUPYING_STATUSES, correctly excluding 'waitlist'/'rejected'. Looks solid; recommend adding a unit test that simulates 5 concurrent transactions to lock the contract.
GDPR /api/gdpr/anonymize: the 400 guard short-circuits before reading the row — good. Recommend logging the user_id who attempted with wrong confirmation to detect probing.
seed_data_creation: Created and DELETED two test events on demo institution (capacity=1) — both cleaned up after tests. Two waitlist test applications remain in the demo institution (waitlist_test@example.com and second test_first_applicant). They are tied to the deleted events; can be removed if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: A10 verified end-to-end on the demo institution (c18a10b9-...). The events API requires `start_datetime`/`end_datetime` strings on POST /{event_id}/dates (NOT `date`). Public events list testid is `public-event-{event_id}` (not `open-event-{id}`). To test waitlist UI: create event capacity=1 + a future date, seed one application via /api/events/public/{inst}/apply to fill it, then visit /events/{inst} — the badge `date-waitlist-{date_id}` and waitlist-notice + 'Přihlásit na čekací listinu' submit will appear; success page shows `waitlist-success-title` 'Jste na čekací listině' with NO QR. For A11, demo is PRO+ so PlanUsageBanner is hidden as expected; /api/plan/usage returns enforced:false and unlimited blocks. For A12, only the 400/401 guards were tested (destructive anonymize on demo was correctly avoided per the review note).
rca of the issue: No issues found. All P0/P1 acceptance criteria from the review request met. Schema mismatch on event-date creation (start_datetime/end_datetime vs date) was a test-side fix only; production frontend already sends the correct shape.
